stories.headline: US-Israel war against Iran: strikes, casualties, and regional escalation

articles.title: Five ways the Iran war could affect you - in charts
articles.description: With fuel and gas prices having risen in recent days, here are some ways the conflict could affect households.

articles.title: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
articles.description: Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

articles.title: Iran retaliates after Israel strikes Beirut and Tehran as war enters Day 7
articles.description: Iran fired missiles toward Israel Friday, Israeli officials say, after Israel launched fresh strikes on Tehran and hit Beirut's southern suburbs overnight.

articles.title: Iran live updates: Ukraine drone specialists going to Middle East to defend US bases
articles.description: President Donald Trump called upon the Iranian Revolutionary Guard, military and police to "lay down their arms" against the U.S. and Israel.

articles.title: WATCH:  US sub sinks Iranian ship by torpedo in Indian Ocean, 1st such attack since WWII
articles.description: A U.S. submarine used a torpedo to sink and Iranian ship in the Indian Ocean Tuesday, the first such attack since WWII , the Pentagon said.

articles.title: Trump says U.S. and Israel intend to "go in and clean out" Iran regime
articles.description: With the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran in its seventh day, Trump says the aim is to "go in and clean out everything," suggesting a regime change objective.

articles.title: Trump says he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader as war expands
articles.description: President Trump said he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader and called the son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei "unacceptable."

articles.title: Analysis Suggests School Was Hit Amid U.S. Strikes on Iranian Naval Base
articles.description: The Feb. 28 school strike in Minab, which killed dozens, including children, appears to have been part of an attack on an adjacent naval base in southern Iran, where officials said U.S. forces were operating.

articles.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
articles.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

articles.title: Trump says he wants Iran's leadership structure gone and has preferences for a 'good leader'
articles.description: In a phone call with NBC News, the president also downplayed the possibility of a ground invasion in Iran.

articles.title: Special Report: NBC News exclusive interview with Iran’s foreign minister on war with U.S.
articles.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi spoke with “NBC Nightly News" anchor Tom Llamas in a video interview from Tehran as U.S. and Israeli bombs continue to hammer the country. Araghchi said that Iran is ready for a U.S. ground invasion of the country as the war between Iran, the U.S. and Israel has quickly spread across the region. He also refused any negotiations with the U.S. and said that Iran had not asked for a ceasefire.

articles.title: Republican-led House declines to constrain Trump's war in Iran, just as Senate did
articles.description: The Republican-controlled Congress narrowly voted against constraining President Donald Trump’s war in Iran.

articles.title: Strikes and explosions across the Middle East
articles.description: As war between Iran, the U.S., and Israel spreads in the Middle East, strikes and explosions cause mass destruction across the region – including in residential areas.

articles.title: Donald Trump Is a Great Man of History

articles.title: Pastors pray for Trump as strikes on Iran continue
articles.description: Video shows pastors praying over US President Donald Trump in the Oval Office amid US strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Huge explosion seen in Tehran as strikes on Iran continue
articles.description: Video from Tehran showed a huge explosion in Iran’s capital as US and Israeli air attacks continued.

articles.title: Is BRICS bloc divided over US-Israel attacks on Iran?

articles.description: Most of the costs are not budgeted for, meaning the Pentagon will need to request more funding soon, researchers say.

articles.title: Tens of thousands in scramble for flights out of Middle East amid Iran war

articles.title: Trump’s war with Iran is expanding. Will others join the fight?
articles.description: The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran is rapidly expanding across the region, sucking in Gulf allies and pulling European countries and Iranian proxies into the fight. Since the joint operation was launched over the weekend, Tehran has retaliated by firing thousands of drones and missiles against Israel and the U.S. interests in Gulf states, further inflaming…

articles.title: The Memo: Polls show deep public skepticism of Trump’s case on Iran
articles.description: Six days after the U.S. attacked Iran as part of a joint operation with Israel, opinion polls reveal grave doubts among the American public about the endeavor. The lack of public support spells political peril for President Trump, even as he enthuses about how well American actions are going, celebrates the killing of Iranian Supreme…

Republican group attacks Thomas Massie for his opposition to Iran war
articles.description: An ad supporting the lawmaker’s rival aired hours after the House rejected a War Powers resolution.

articles.title: Succession, Tehran style
articles.description: Within hours of Israeli and American attacks on the government and military infrastructure of Iran, President Donald Trump made it clear the United States’ goal was not to enforce its view of Iran’s future. Instead, Trump insisted the joint military operation offered the people of Iran seeking a secular government an opportunity to establish their […]

articles.title: Iran war certain to impact wind-down of US military aid to Israel
articles.description: The joint Israel-U.S. war against Iran could complicate fledgling plans to end American financial aid to the Jewish state over the next decade. Since Feb. 28, the allies have coordinated military actions against the Shia Islamic theocracy, targeting its leadership, nuclear and ballistic missile program, and armed forces. Given the Iran war, Israeli Prime Minister […]

articles.title: Democratic divisions brew over funding Trump’s Iran war: ‘Going to be a need’
articles.description: Democrats are looking ahead to the next battle after losing a fight in Congress this week to rein in President Donald Trump’s war against Iran: Making sure he doesn’t have the money to wage it. Democrats are vowing to wield their Senate filibuster power against more spending to fund the president’s war efforts after both […]

articles.title: The global economy sure doesn’t think Trump started World War III
articles.description: Ask left-wing and far-right politicos across the planet about President Donald Trump‘s decision to launch Operation Epic Fury against the Iranian dictatorship, and they’ll warn he has brought us to the precipice of World War III. Investors across the globe see the situation differently. American equities and bond markets are mostly muted since the joint […]

articles.title: Trump’s Mideast revolution: A chance to transform the world’s most turbulent region
articles.description: The most revolutionary force in the Middle East is President Donald Trump. America and its ally Israel, achieving unparalleled levels of military and intelligence integration, have decapitated the Iranian terrorist regime, devastated its command structures, destroyed its weapons, and sunk a substantial proportion of its navy — they’re “at the bottom of the sea,” Trump […]

articles.title: The Iranian Grand Prix
articles.description: With Iran lobbing suicide drones and ballistic missiles at every military base and apartment complex from Muscat to Tel Aviv, the most important question facing the Middle East is: What does this mean for the Bahrain Grand Prix? OK, OK, Formula 1 racing is perhaps not the greatest concern facing the world right now, but […]

analysis.summary: The United States and Israel launched a joint military operation against Iran beginning around February 28, 2026, targeting its leadership, military infrastructure, nuclear program, and navy. Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed, and Iran retaliated with thousands of missiles and drones against Israel and US interests in the Gulf, while the conflict spread to involve Lebanon, Azerbaijan, Cyprus, and Gulf states. The war triggered global energy supply disruptions, congressional debates over war powers, and deep divisions over whether the operation represents a strategic success or a dangerous escalation.
agreements: The US and Israel launched coordinated military strikes against Iran beginning around late February 2026
Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at Israel and US bases in the region
Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in the strikes
A US submarine sank an Iranian ship with a torpedo in the Indian Ocean, the first such attack since WWII
The US has destroyed much of Iran's navy
President Trump publicly expressed regime change objectives and stated he wants a role in choosing Iran's next leader
Both the Senate and the Republican-led House voted against War Powers resolutions to constrain Trump's military actions
A school in Minab, Iran was struck alongside an adjacent naval base, killing civilians including children
The war caused major energy supply disruptions affecting Asia and global markets
The conflict spread regionally, with strikes in Lebanon, Iranian retaliation against Gulf states, and Azerbaijan accusing Iran of a drone attack
Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi said Iran was ready for a ground invasion and refused negotiations or ceasefire

disagreements.topic: Casualty count at the Minab school strike

positions.position: Reports Iranian authorities say the attack killed 168 people
Reports the strike 'killed dozens, including children,' a significantly lower figure

disagreements.topic: Overall framing of the war's legitimacy and strategic wisdom

positions.position: Frames Trump as 'A Great Man of History' who is ending a forever war, not starting one
Frames the operation as 'a chance to transform the world's most turbulent region' and celebrates military success
Frames it as 'America and Israel remind the world how wars are fought to victory' while cautioning about applying Iraq's lessons
Emphasizes polls showing 'deep public skepticism' of Trump's case for war and political peril
Emphasizes the $3.7 billion cost, civilian impact, regional destabilization, and anger in countries like Cyprus being dragged into conflict

disagreements.topic: Whether this war parallels the 2003 Iraq invasion

positions.position: Niall Ferguson argues 'Khamenei May Be Gone, but Iran 2026 Is Not Iraq 2003'
Acknowledges 'Iran is not Iraq 2.0' but focuses on applying Iraq's lessons to avoid past mistakes
Downplays Iraq parallels, focusing on the opportunity for democratic transformation in Iran

disagreements.topic: Whether the war represents an escalation risk or a contained operation

positions.position: Argues 'the global economy sure doesn't think Trump started World War III' and that markets are muted
Reports the war is 'rapidly expanding across the region, sucking in Gulf allies and pulling European countries into the fight'
Highlights tens of thousands scrambling for flights, Qatar warning energy exports will halt, and BRICS divisions
Reports Pakistan-Afghanistan border clashes, 100,000 displaced, Asian fuel shortages, and Gulf energy export warnings

disagreements.topic: Responsibility for the school strike and its characterization

positions.position: Analysis suggests the school was hit 'amid U.S. strikes on an Iranian naval base,' attributing the strike to US forces operating in the area
Reports the school and 'nearby military base' were struck multiple times based on satellite imagery, without explicitly assigning blame in the headline

disagreements.topic: Whether this is primarily America's or Israel's war

positions.position: One article explicitly argues 'This Isn't Israel's War. It's America's'
Frames it as a 'U.S.-Israeli war on Iran' with shared objectives
Consistently describes it as 'US-Israel attacks on Iran,' presenting both as co-equal aggressors

disagreements.topic: International allied support for the operation

positions.position: Reports NATO Chief Rutte says 'Trump's Iran Mission Has Broad Allied Support'
Reports Spain's government denies cooperating with US operations, contradicting the White House, and highlights Spain's PM as Europe's most vocal critic
Reports France deployed its nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to the Mediterranean, suggesting a defensive rather than supportive posture

uniqueAngles.angle: Reports that Ukraine drone specialists are being sent to the Middle East to help defend US bases, a unique detail about cross-conflict resource sharing
Secured an exclusive interview with Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi from Tehran during active bombing, providing the Iranian government's direct perspective
Reports on anger in Cyprus over UK military bases being used, with protests over the island being dragged into the conflict after an attack on a British military site
Uniquely covers BRICS bloc divisions over the war, noting India's cautious approach as BRICS chair
Reports wealthy travelers paying up to $200,000 to charter flights out of the Middle East
Quantifies the war's cost at $3.7 billion in the first 100 hours, citing a think tank
Reports South Korea and US militaries are discussing moving Patriot missile systems to the Iran war theater
Reports India invoked emergency powers and ordered refiners to boost LPG output due to energy supply disruptions
Covers hundreds of ships dropping anchor in the Gulf, illustrating the war's effect on maritime shipping
Reports on Pakistani and Afghan border forces clashing amid UN reporting 100,000 displaced by the war
Covers a Republican group attacking Rep. Thomas Massie specifically for opposing the Iran war, with an ad supporting his rival airing hours after the House War Powers vote
Reports the war complicates existing plans to wind down US military aid to Israel over the next decade
Covers the impact on the Bahrain Grand Prix and Formula 1 racing as a unique cultural angle
Argues the Iran war validates Trump's creation of Space Force, citing clear benefits from the newest military branch
Zineb Riboua argues the Iran strike is primarily about China, framing it in a broader geopolitical context
Covers the 'posthumous makeover' of Khamenei, examining how his image is being reshaped after death
Analyzes how the Iran conflict is eroding Russia's global influence, with Trump 'out-Putining Putin'
Provides consumer-focused analysis of how the war affects households through fuel and gas price charts
Reports on Democratic divisions over using the Senate filibuster to block war funding
Reports Gulf states claim to have shot down more than 1,500 Iranian missiles and drones
Reports Trump personally framed Khamenei's killing as retribution, saying 'I Got Khamenei First, Tried to Kill Me Twice'
Reports Trump downplayed the possibility of a ground invasion in Iran during a phone call with NBC News

articles.description: Mayor Jacob Frey of Minneapolis said “Good riddance” after Ms. Noem’s ouster. Gov. Tim Walz and others said they still wanted a “complete overhaul” of the department.

articles.title: Trump fires DHS Secretary Kristi Noem
articles.description: President Trump has fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem, the first cabinet secretary he’s fired in his second term. The president announced Sen. Markywane Mullin as Noem’s replacement, pending Senate confirmation. NBC News’ Peter Alexander reports.

articles.title: Noem’s tumultuous tenure at DHS marked by controversies, confrontations
articles.description: The toppling of Kristi Noem on Thursday marks the first firing of the second Trump presidency and the exit of a face at the center of President Trump’s controversial pledge to carry out mass deportations. During her tenure as secretary of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Noem faced Democratic threats to impeach her as she surged…

articles.title: Hegseth on Noem’s ouster: ‘We all serve at the pleasure of the president’
articles.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday that Kristi Noem did an “excellent job” at the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), hours after President Trump announced he had tapped Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.) to replace her as the department’s secretary. “We all serve at the pleasure of the president,” Hegseth told reporters during a briefing on…

articles.title: 'Good riddance': Dems cheer Noem’s ouster — and call for more departures
articles.description: Democratic lawmakers said Stephen Miller and Pam Bondi should be next after President Donald Trump announced Kristi Noem would be stepping aside as DHS secretary.

articles.title: ‘When, not if’: Trump insiders say Noem was long on chopping block
articles.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem’s clash with GOP senators this week was the final straw for President Donald Trump, though insiders say her fate had been sealed long before Tuesday’s hearing. Two days after that hearing, Trump announced via social media that Noem would be replaced at DHS by Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-OK), effective March […]

analysis.summary: President Trump fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem on Thursday, making her the first cabinet secretary dismissed in his second term. Trump nominated Senator Markwayne Mullin (R-OK) as her replacement, pending Senate confirmation. The firing followed mounting controversies, including a contentious congressional hearing where Noem was grilled about a $220 million self-deportation ad campaign.
agreements: Trump fired Kristi Noem as DHS Secretary, the first cabinet firing of his second term
Trump nominated Senator Markwayne Mullin (R-OK) to replace Noem as DHS Secretary
Mullin's appointment requires Senate confirmation
A contentious congressional hearing earlier in the week, where Noem faced tough questioning from Republicans, was a key factor in the firing
A $220 million advertising/self-deportation ad campaign was a major point of controversy
Noem had helped advance Trump's deportation and border enforcement agenda during her tenure
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth commented that 'we all serve at the pleasure of the president' and called Noem's work 'excellent'

disagreements.topic: Primary reason for Noem's firing

positions.position: Trump suggested in a phone call that the dismissal was less about Noem's performance and more about elevating Sen. Mullin to his Cabinet
Trump insiders say Noem's fate had been sealed long before the hearing, calling it a matter of 'when, not if'
Noem's testimony about the $220 million ad campaign may have been the 'final straw' for Trump
Framed as firing after 'mounting criticism over her leadership'

disagreements.topic: Assessment of Noem's tenure at DHS

positions.position: Noem was 'never well-suited to the job' and made a serious role into 'something out of Veep'
Characterized her tenure as involving 'mayhem, waste and abuse'
Described a 'tumultuous tenure' marked by controversies and confrontations, but noted she was central to Trump's mass deportation pledge
Acknowledged Noem 'boosted deportations' but noted her controversies frustrated Trump
Noem 'helped fulfill his border pledges' but 'drew negative attention to his administration'

disagreements.topic: Noem's post-firing role

positions.position: Reports that Noem was assigned as a special envoy
Do not mention any new role for Noem after her firing

Focused on Democrats cheering Noem's ouster and calling for additional firings of Stephen Miller and Pam Bondi
Covered Minnesota Governor Tim Walz and Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey welcoming the firing, with Frey saying 'Good riddance' and officials calling for a 'complete overhaul' of DHS
Uniquely reported that Noem was reassigned as a special envoy rather than simply fired
Had a direct phone call with Trump in which he said he 'wasn't thrilled' with Noem's $220M ad campaign, offering a firsthand presidential perspective
Cited Trump insiders saying Noem had been on the 'chopping block' long before the hearing, suggesting the decision was preordained
Included broader context about Noem's past controversies, including the infamous dog-killing incident and luxury jet use
Noted that Democrats had threatened to impeach Noem during her tenure

stories.headline: Israel strikes Lebanon, Beirut evacuation orders issued amid Hezbollah conflict

articles.title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
articles.description: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.

articles.title: Thousands displaced in Beirut after Israeli forced evacuation order
articles.description: Thousands displaced in Beirut after Israeli forced evacuation order

articles.title: More than 120 killed in Israel’s Lebanon attacks as Beirut, south, east hit
articles.description: Lebanon's Hezbollah group urges Israelis to evacuate border areas as Israel continues to bomb the country.

articles.title: Israel strikes Beirut’s southern suburbs after issuing a blanket evacuation order - AP News

articles.title: Israel pounds Hezbollah targets, daring Lebanon to reclaim sovereignty from Iran-backed terror proxy
articles.description: Lebanon's government calls for Hezbollah disarmament amid renewed Israel conflict, as international leaders including Macron warn against broader war.

analysis.summary: Israel has launched intensified strikes against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including Beirut's southern suburbs, after issuing blanket evacuation orders to residents in Beirut and dozens of Lebanese border villages. The attacks have killed over 120 people and displaced thousands, while Lebanon's government faces pressure to disarm Hezbollah amid the escalating conflict.
agreements: Israel struck targets in Beirut's southern suburbs and other areas of Lebanon
Israel issued evacuation orders to Lebanese civilians in multiple areas including Beirut and southern border villages
Large numbers of Lebanese civilians have been displaced by the strikes and evacuation orders
Lebanon's government is facing questions about disarming Hezbollah
The strikes are part of an ongoing Israel-Hezbollah conflict

disagreements.topic: Framing of the evacuation orders

positions.position: Describes them as 'Israeli forced evacuation orders,' emphasizing the coercive nature and the displacement of thousands
Uses more neutral language: Israel 'issuing a blanket evacuation order' and 'warns residents to evacuate'
Does not highlight the evacuation orders or displacement in its framing at all

disagreements.topic: Primary framing of the story

positions.position: Frames the story around the humanitarian toll: over 120 killed, thousands displaced, emphasizing the impact on Lebanese civilians
Frames the story around a political 'tipping point' for Lebanon, focusing on the question of whether the government will disarm Hezbollah
Frames the story as Israel 'daring Lebanon to reclaim sovereignty' from an 'Iran-backed terror proxy,' casting the strikes as a challenge to Lebanese governance
